title: thelypodium integrifolium subsp. complanatum
intro: Thelypodium integrifolium subsp. complanatum, also known as low chamisa, is a subspecies of the Thelypodium integrifolium plant, a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Thelypodium integrifolium subsp. complanatum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
